# ha-frontend 20260527.6 — LCARS Theme Impact Analysis

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Release tag | `20260527.6` |
| Type | Stable |
| Published | 2026-06-11T13:43:23Z |
| Analyzed | 2026-06-14T18:33:14Z |
| Changed files | 20 |

## Release Notes

- Open more-info from energy pie chart legend, enlarge legend toggle on touch (@MindFreeze - #52506)
- Fix disabled action items icon button color in hui edit mode (@jpbede - #52507)
- Fix scrolling behavior for auto-height data table (@jpbede - #52508)
- Add condition live testing to action conditions too (@bramkragten - #52511)
- Fix camera/image proxy URLs sent with token=undefined (@bramkragten - #52514)
- Show condition row icon on mobile in visibility editor (@marcinbauer-ohf - #52527)
- Don't try to load brand images without a token (@bramkragten - #52532)
- Filter expired camera/image proxy requests in service worker (@bramkragten - #52534)
- Gate more info "Add to" button to admins (@timmo001 - #52547)

---

## HIGH IMPACT

No high-impact breaking changes identified in this release. No CSS custom properties were renamed or removed, no web component element names changed, no shadow DOM structure was restructured, and no slots or parts were removed.

---

## MEDIUM IMPACT

### Component: `hui-root` — Edit Mode Disabled Icon Button Color

**What changed:** `src/panels/lovelace/hui-root.ts` added a new scoped rule inside `.edit-mode`:

```css
.edit-mode .action-items ha-icon-button[disabled] {
  --ha-color-on-disabled-quiet: color-mix(
    in srgb,
    var(--app-header-edit-text-color, #fff) 50%,
    transparent
  );
}
```

This introduces `--ha-color-on-disabled-quiet` as a new token that controls the icon color of disabled `ha-icon-button` elements inside `.action-items` during dashboard edit mode.

**Effect on ha-lcars:** The LCARS theme sets edit-mode toolbar colors in `card-mod-root-yaml` under `div.edit-mode`:

```yaml
.toolbar .action-items {
  --text-primary-color: var(--lcars-ui-primary-text);
}
```

The theme does **not** currently set `--ha-color-on-disabled-quiet`. HA's new fallback is `color-mix(in srgb, var(--app-header-edit-text-color, #fff) 50%, transparent)`, which will resolve to a 50%-transparent white. On the LCARS edit-mode header (which uses `app-header-edit-background-color: transparent` and `app-header-text-color` derived from `--lcars-ui-app-header-text-color`), disabled action buttons will render at half-opacity white rather than a LCARS-appropriate muted color. This is visually tolerable but inconsistent with the LCARS palette.

**Suggested fix:** Add to the `div.edit-mode` block in `card-mod-root-yaml`:

```css
.toolbar .action-items ha-icon-button[disabled] {
  --ha-color-on-disabled-quiet: color-mix(
    in srgb,
    var(--lcars-ui-primary-text) 40%,
    transparent
  );
}
```

This replaces the white fallback with a muted version of the LCARS header text color.

---

### Component: `ha-card-condition-editor` — `.icon-badge-wrapper` Visibility Breakpoint Removed

**What changed:** `src/panels/lovelace/editor/conditions/ha-card-condition-editor.ts` removed the `@media (min-width: 870px)` guard around `.icon-badge-wrapper`. Previously `.icon-badge-wrapper` was `display: none` on narrow screens and only shown at ≥870 px. Now it is unconditionally `display: inline-flex` at all viewport widths.

**Effect on ha-lcars:** The LCARS theme targets `.icon-badge-wrapper` indirectly through its global `secondary-text-color` and `--lcars-primary-text` cascade. The component's internal CSS now sets `.icon-badge-wrapper { color: var(--secondary-text-color); opacity: 0.9; }` at all screen sizes. Since the LCARS theme sets `secondary-text-color: var(--lcars-primary-text)`, the icon badge will now be visible on mobile at the LCARS text color, which is correct and acceptable. However, if any LCARS card-mod rule ever hid or repositioned `.icon-badge-wrapper` assuming it was absent on mobile, that rule would now produce unexpected layout.

**Current LCARS exposure:** No direct selector for `.icon-badge-wrapper` exists in the LCARS theme source, so there is no broken rule. The change is noted because on narrow screens (≤870 px) the condition row icon badge will now appear where it previously did not, potentially affecting layout density in the automation/visibility editor.

**Suggested fix:** No immediate action required. Monitor the condition editor layout on mobile; if the newly always-visible icon badge crowds the LCARS-styled condition rows, add a targeted rule in `card-mod-config-yaml`:

```css
"ha-card-condition-editor $": |
  .icon-badge-wrapper {
    opacity: 0.9;
    color: var(--lcars-primary-text);
  }
```

---

## LOW IMPACT

### Component: `hui-energy-devices-graph-card` — New `click-label-for-more-info` Attribute and Legend Toggle Sizing

**What changed:** `src/panels/lovelace/cards/energy/hui-energy-devices-graph-card.ts` added the Boolean attribute `click-label-for-more-info` to `ha-chart-base`, enabling clicking a pie chart legend entry to open the more-info dialog. It also enlarged the legend toggle hit target on touch devices (PR #52506).

**Effect on ha-lcars:** No LCARS CSS rule targets `hui-energy-devices-graph-card` internals, `ha-chart-base`, or the chart legend elements. The more-info dialog that opens on legend click will use the existing `card-mod-more-info-yaml` styles, which are already defined and appropriate.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed. Optionally, if the theme wishes to style the newly interactive legend items to match the LCARS palette, a rule targeting `hui-energy-devices-graph-card ha-chart-base` could be added, but this is not required.

---

### Component: `ha-more-info-dialog` — "Add to" Button Gated to Admins

**What changed:** `src/dialogs/more-info/ha-more-info-add-to.ts` now gates the "Add to dashboard / area / label" button in the more-info dialog to admin users only (PR #52547).

**Effect on ha-lcars:** The `card-mod-more-info-yaml` block styles the more-info dialog host but does not specifically target the "Add to" button or its container. Non-admin users will simply not see the button; no layout shift large enough to break LCARS framing is expected.

**Suggested fix:** No fix needed.

---
